question: Comment structurer les error boundaries dans une app React ?

principles.text: 3 layers: global (catch-all reload), per-route (isole crash module du shell), per-feature (optionnel, sections critiques). react-error-boundary package. NE PAS wrapper chaque composant — granularite excessive est contre-productive. Le global empeche le white screen, le per-route preserve la navigation.

sources.name: React Official Docs — Error Boundaries
sources.quote: Error boundaries catch errors in their child component tree

sources.name: React 19 Release Notes
sources.quote: Improved error reporting and boundary behavior in React 19

sources.name: react-error-boundary npm — 10M+ dl/week
sources.quote: De facto standard for declarative error boundaries

sources.name: Kent C. Dodds — Use react-error-boundary
sources.quote: Layer boundaries at route and feature level, not per-component

sources.name: React Router errorElement
sources.quote: Built-in per-route error handling via errorElement prop
